uncertain_words.reason: Critical symptom in 71-year-old smoker — aetiology determines urgency
Chronicity affects urgency and management pathway
Smoking pack-year history needed for COPD staging

subjective.chief_complaint: Progressive exertional dyspnoea with chronic dry cough — 71-year-old long-term smoker
history_of_present_illness: Exertional dyspnoea (ضيقة في النفس) — onset من شوية (duration unclear)
Worsens on ambulation and stair climbing
Age 71, long-term smoker (من زمان — pack-year history unknown)

assessment.summary: Likely COPD exacerbation or progression given age, smoking history, and exertional dyspnoea pattern. Lung malignancy must be excluded. Heart failure in differential.
unsupported_or_inferred: COPD diagnosis inferred from smoking + exertional dyspnoea — spirometry required for staging
Malignancy excluded only by imaging — critical red flag in 71-year-old smoker with dry cough

recommended_clarifications: CT chest if X-ray inconclusive or malignancy suspected
SpO₂ at rest and on exertion
patient_instructions_draft: Smoking cessation — refer to cessation programme
Review influenza and pneumococcal vaccination status

claims.text: COPD most likely given age, smoking history, and exertional dyspnoea pattern
Lung malignancy must be excluded — 71-year-old long-term smoker with dry cough
Heart failure in differential — exertional dyspnoea with bilateral symptoms possible

transcription_quality.note: Mixed Egyptian-Syrian dialect handled, some tokens uncertain

dialect_coverage.note: شوية and من زمان duration ambiguity both flagged

clinical_extraction.note: COPD pattern identified, red flags noted

evidence_support.note: Malignancy exclusion requires imaging — not supported by transcript alone

completeness.note: Pack-year history, SpO₂, spirometry all missing

self_eval.summary: Needs review. Mixed dialect and critical red flags (malignancy) require physician confirmation before note finalises.

physician_questions.title: Dyspnoea onset
physician_questions.question: How long has the dyspnoea (ضيقة في النفس) been present? شوية is ambiguous.
physician_questions.reason: Chronicity determines if this is acute exacerbation vs progressive COPD — changes urgency

physician_questions.title: Smoking pack-year history
physician_questions.question: Patient says بدخن من زمان — can you estimate pack-years or when they started smoking?
physician_questions.reason: Pack-year history is required for COPD staging (GOLD classification) and malignancy risk stratification

physician_questions.title: Haemoptysis
physician_questions.question: Has the patient had any blood in sputum (نفث دم)?
physician_questions.reason: Haemoptysis in 71-year-old smoker with dry cough raises immediate malignancy concern
